transcript_facts:
- [Assessment] he's a 49 year old , uh hm , with hypertension and palpitations .
- [Assessment] uh , if the new med does n't work , the next up is going to be the resistant hypertension clinic .
- [Assessment] so , there they will look into secondary causes of your hypertension .
- [Assessment] mr. roberts presents with resistant hypertension .
- [Findings] please copy forward his thyroid profile from march 1st , 2021 .
- [Findings] uh , so tell me about the blood pressure then . well , i've had issues with my blood pressure since i was young .
- [Findings] yeah , i thought it was getting better , and , uh , i have a new primary doctor , and she put me on a new medication about a month ago .
- [Findings] the benicar hct .
- [Findings] are you checking your blood pressure at home , and if yes , what numbers are you seeing ?
- [Findings] um , i'll give you some information on the dash eating plan , which is the only eating plan that has been shown to lower blood pressure .
- [Findings] uh , your potassium one week ago was 4.0 .
- [Findings] uh , four is good for your potassium level , some people have what is called resistant hypertension that is driven by something else in their body , uh , often it is a form , uh , hormonal response . but , for the two out of 10 , there is something else driving it , uh , parathyroid levels or something called your adrenal glands overproduce , um , aldosterone , which we call hyperaldosterone .
- [Findings] um , in these cases , there's medicine called , uh , spironolactone , that is very effective at dropping the ald- aldosterone level , and your blood pressure , and i think we should give that a try .
- [Findings] and , the spironolactone . one issue is that they can affect your electrolyte balance .
- [Findings] um , second thing is the spironolactone can raise your potassium too far , it's rare , but it can . uh , you're starting with your potassium at four , so that gives us some room .
- [Findings] and then the third thing is that spironolactone combined with hydrocholorozide- chlorothiazide can cause your sodium levels to go down . but , it does mean that you will need to do blood tests in two weeks to make sure that it's okay .
- [Findings] um , are there any other side effects i need to worry about ? this is a rare side effect , um , called gynecomastia , and if it happens , you just cut the medication and you let me know , but it is very uncommon .
- [Findings] uh , we'll keep working at it until your blood pressure gets better . um , it can be tricky when your blood pressure starts to go up at a young age though , it is important to get it under control because it can lead to your heart getting bigger or enlarged .
- [Findings] um , the american heart association has a website called heart.org , it has a lot of really good information about blood pressure to teach- to teach you about it and how to control your diet with exercise . um , if you do your treadmill routinely for 30 minutes or so most days , that could be seven to 10 points off your blood pressure .
- [Findings] so , we'll try the spironolactone and schedule a followup in two weeks to check your blood pressure and electrolytes .
- [Findings] so , that procedure has been shown to lower blood pressure by 10 points .
- [Findings] i have added spironolactone to his regiment . we will need to monitor his potassium and sodium . he will have a metabolic panel and blood pressure check in two weeks . uh , next medication would be carvedilol , uh , which i try to avoid giving , it's potential for erectile dysfunction . i have talked to the patient about that , and the potential side effects of spironolactone , including the gynecomastia and electrolyte disturbances . if he is not controlled on spironolactone , the next step would be sending him to resistant hypertension clinic .
- [History of Present Illness] so , i saw you were recently in the emergency room with high blood pressure and some palpitations .
- [History of Present Illness] so , i think it was probably more of a panic attack than anything else .
- [History of Present Illness] a lot of my family does have high blood pressure .
- [History of Present Illness] it has hydrochlorothiazide in it .
- [History of Present Illness] are you still taking the amlodipine , 10 milligrams daily ?
- [History of Present Illness] i'm just gon na look for some swelling .
- [History of Present Illness] so , in terms of getting that pressure down , you're on three meds that are maxed out and your blood pressure is still not down .
- [History of Present Illness] if i were to take 10 people under the age of 50 who have high blood pressure who developed it at a young age , eight out of 10 would respond to reduced salt in their diet with a lower blood pressure .
- [History of Present Illness] so , let's discuss the piss- the possible risk of , uh , being on hydrochlorothiazide , which is the fluid med that you're now on .
- [History of Present Illness] um , they can also cause dehydration . if you do not hydrate , and you get dehydrated , you will know it because you will get dizzy standing up .
- [History of Present Illness] uh , dizziness possibly , as well as , uh , one in 100 guys might develop tenderness in their chest , swollen breasts , or enlarged breasts .
- [History of Present Illness] um , same thing that happens when you lift weights and your muscles get bigger , uh , and if your heart pumps against high blood pressure , it can eventually lead to heart failure . yeah , so getting that under control is really important to help prevent kidney failure , strokes , and- and things like that- that you're at high risk for with high blood pressure .
- [History of Present Illness] they have access to newer procedure based technologies that can lower your blood pressure without medication , uh , for example , there is something called a renal , uh , denervation , uh , where they go inside the artery that feeds your kidney with almost like a little coil and heat that artery up .
- [History of Present Illness] uh , but we'll cross that road , uh , when we need to , and let's just- let's just see how you do with the addition of the medication .
- [History of Present Illness] he's on three meds and maximum dose .
